dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9661 IH: Expedited Access to Biosimilars Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Langworthy (for himself and Ms. Schrier) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Public Health Service Act to codify that the default expectation for licensure of biological products as biosimilar does not include clinical studies assessing pharmacodynamics or comparative clinical efficacy, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Expedited Access to Biosimilars Act.

section.header: Assessment of pharmacodynamics or efficacy in clinical studies required for licensure of biological products as biosimilar

subsection.text: Section 351(k)(2)(A) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 262(k)(2)(A)) is amended—

clause.text: by striking item (aa) or (cc) and inserting item (aa), (cc), or (dd); and
by striking and at the end; and

subparagraph.text: by striking item (cc) and inserting the following:

item.text: an assessment of pharmacokinetics and immunogenicity (which may rely on, or consist of, a clinical pharmacokinetics study or studies or a study or studies described in item (aa) or (dd), as appropriate); and

subject to clause (iv), an additional clinical study or studies in 1 or more appropriate conditions of use for which the reference product is licensed and intended to be used and for which licensure is sought for the biological product;

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

clause.header: Limitation on requiring additional clinical study or studies
clause.text: The Secretary may only require an additional clinical study or studies described in clause (i)(I)(dd), including a study or studies that include an assessment of pharmacodynamics or efficacy, if the Secretary determines that such study or studies are necessary, in combination with the other studies described in clause (i)(I), to demonstrate biosimilarity and provides written notice of such determination to the sponsor of the proposed biosimilar biological product. Such written notice shall be provided not later than—

subclause.text: the date on which the Secretary grants a request for a biosimilar biological product development meeting from such sponsor, unless the Secretary describes in writing why an assessment of the need for such study or studies cannot be made at that time; or

if no request for a biosimilar biological product development meeting is submitted or if the Secretary was unable to make the assessment under subclause (I), the date that is 60 days after the date of the submission of an application under this subsection.

subsection.header: Repeal of requirement relating To conduct of reviews
subsection.text: Section 351(k)(5) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 262(k)(5)) is amended—

The amendments made by subsection (a) shall apply with respect to an application submitted under section 351(k) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 262(k)) on or after the date of enactment of this Act.
